content.text: Elasticity is the ability of a material to return to its original shape and size after the removal of an external force.

content.name: Stress
content.note: Internal restoring force per unit area. Unit: Pascal (Pa). Types: Normal, Shear, and Bulk Stress.

content.name: Strain
content.note: Dimensionless quantity. Types: Longitudinal, Shear, and Volumetric Strain.

content.text: Within the elastic limit, Stress is directly proportional to Strain ($\text{Stress} \propto \text{Strain}$).
Elastic Behavior: Material returns to original shape. Plastic Behavior: Material undergoes permanent deformation.

content.label: ⚠️ Breaking Point
content.text: If the applied force exceeds the elastic limit, the material reaches its breaking point and fails/fractures.
